mainmatter.p: Let \({\left(V,I,\otimes,\alpha,\lambda,\rho\right)}\) be a monoidal category. A category \(C\) enriched in \(V\) is defined by the following structure and axioms:

ol.li: for any two objects \(x,y\), an object \(\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(x,y\right)}\in V\);
for every object \(x\), a morphism \(I\xrightarrow{i_{x}}\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(x,x\right)}\) in \(V\);
for all objects \(x,y,z\), a morphism \(\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(x,y\right)}\otimes\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(y,z\right)}\xrightarrow{c_{x,y,z}}\mathbf{hom}_{C}{\left(x,z\right)}\) in \(V\).
such that the following diagrams commute in \(V\):

mainmatter.p: A monoidal category is a category \(C\) equipped with the following structure and axioms:

ol.li: a functor \(C\times C\xrightarrow{\otimes}C\) called the tensor product;
an object \(I\in C\) called the tensor unit;
a natural isomorphism \({\left(x\otimes y\right)}\otimes z \xrightarrow{\alpha_{x,y,z}} x\otimes{\left(y\otimes z\right)}\) called the associator;
a natural isomorphism \(I\otimes x \xrightarrow{\lambda_{x}}x\) called the left unitor;
a natural isomorphism \(x\otimes I \xrightarrow{\rho_{x}}x\) called the right unitor;
such that the triangle below commutes:

mainmatter.p: A category with zero morphisms is defined to be a category enriched in the category of pointed sets and strict maps. The distinguished point of \(\mathbf{hom}_{\mathcal{A}}{\left(A,B\right)}\) shall be written \(0\).
